footer.tagline: Self-hosted error monitoring for modern applications.

hero.subtitle: Self-hosted Sentry alternative. Track, debug, and resolve errors in your applications with powerful stack traces, real-time alerts, and beautiful dashboards.
hero.trustBar: Trusted by developers at startups and enterprises

features.title: Everything you need to
features.subtitle: Powerful features to help you track, analyze, and resolve errors in your applications.

0.title: Real-time Error Tracking
0.description: Capture errors as they happen with full stack traces, request data, and environment context.

1.title: Beautiful Dashboard
1.description: Monitor your application health with charts, trends, and actionable insights at a glance.

2.title: Multi-tenant Support
2.description: Organize errors by organizations and projects. Invite team members with role-based access.

3.title: SDK Ecosystem
3.description: First-class support for Symfony, Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Hono, Fastify and more.

4.title: Self-hosted
4.description: Keep your data private. Deploy on your own infrastructure with Docker in minutes.

5.title: Secure by Default
5.description: No third-party data sharing. Your error data stays on your servers, always.

howItWorks.title: Up and running in
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps to start monitoring errors in your applications.

0.title: Install the SDK
0.description: Add ErrorWatch to your project with a single command. Supports npm, yarn, bun, and composer.

1.title: Configure your endpoint
1.description: Point the SDK to your self-hosted ErrorWatch server or our cloud offering.

2.title: Monitor and resolve
2.description: Errors are captured automatically. View them in your dashboard and resolve issues fast.

pricing.title: Simple,
pricing.subtitle: Start free with self-hosting, or let us handle the infrastructure.

testimonials.title: Loved by
testimonials.subtitle: See what developers are saying about ErrorWatch.

0.quote: ErrorWatch has completely transformed how we handle production errors. The self-hosted option means we keep full control of our data.
0.author: Sarah Chen

1.quote: Finally, a Sentry alternative that's actually easy to self-host. Setup took less than 10 minutes with Docker.
1.author: Marcus Johnson

2.quote: The multi-tenant support is perfect for our agency. We can monitor all client projects from a single dashboard.
2.author: Emily Rodriguez

cta.title: Ready to ship with confidence?

crons.title: Cron Monitors
crons.description: Monitor your scheduled jobs and detect missed executions
crons.noMonitorsDescription: Create a monitor or send your first check-in via the SDK
crons.name: Name
crons.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this monitor?

guide.passiveTitle: ErrorWatch does NOT run your crons
guide.passiveDescription: This is passive monitoring, like Sentry Crons or Cronitor. Your cron jobs keep running where they already are (crontab, Laravel Scheduler, Kubernetes CronJob...). You just add a small HTTP call in your code so ErrorWatch can track executions and alert you when something goes wrong.
guide.heroDescription: Get alerted when your scheduled tasks fail or miss their execution window. ErrorWatch detects missed check-ins and sends notifications through your configured alert channels.
guide.howItWorksSubtitle: Your server runs the cron, ErrorWatch watches and alerts.
guide.step1Description: Your cron jobs send a simple HTTP request to ErrorWatch before and after execution.
guide.step2Description: ErrorWatch records every check-in with status, duration, and metadata. Monitors are auto-created on first check-in.
guide.step3Description: If a job fails or misses its schedule, you get notified via email, Slack, Discord, Telegram, or as a GitHub/GitLab issue.
guide.statusMissed: Job did not check-in within tolerance
guide.sdkDescription: Add cron monitoring to your application in minutes. Choose your language below.
guide.autoCreateDescription: You don't need to create monitors manually. The first check-in with a new slug automatically creates the monitor. You can then configure the schedule and tolerance from the dashboard.
guide.missedDetectionDescription: When you set a cron schedule on a monitor, ErrorWatch calculates the next expected check-in. If no check-in arrives within the tolerance window, it's marked as missed and alerts are triggered.
guide.missedExample: Schedule: every hour (0 * * * *), Tolerance: 5 min. If the job doesn't check-in by :05 past the hour, it's marked as missed.
guide.apiCheckinDescription: Send a check-in for a cron monitor. Authenticated via X-API-Key header.
guide.readyDescription: Create your first monitor manually, or just send a check-in from your code and the monitor will be auto-created.
